title: Choosing a Path: University or Vocational School?
passage:
Choosing a Path: University or Vocational School?

After finishing high school, young people must make an important choice. Some students choose to go to university. This is called higher education. Other students choose to go to a vocational school. This is also called job training. Both paths are good, but they are very different.

University education usually lasts for three to four years. Students study subjects like history, science, or business. They spend a lot of time reading books, writing essays, and listening to lectures in big classrooms. This path is perfect for people who want to become doctors, teachers, or managers. However, university can be very expensive, and students do not learn many hands-on skills.

Vocational training is different. These schools focus on practical skills. Students learn how to do a specific job. For example, they can study to become electricians, car mechanics, chefs, or plumbers. These courses are much shorter, usually taking only one or two years. Students do not write many essays. Instead, they practice their skills in workshops with real tools.

There are many benefits to vocational training. First, it is usually much cheaper than university. Second, because the courses are short, students can finish quickly and start earning money. Today, many companies need workers with good practical skills, so it is easy for vocational graduates to find a job.

Let's look at two friends, Tom and Anna. Tom went to university to study computer science. He studied for four years and now works in an office. Anna went to a vocational school to study cooking. She studied for one year and now works as a chef in a restaurant. Both of them are very happy with their choices.

In conclusion, both choices are excellent. University is great for people who love academic study and want professional careers. Vocational school is perfect for people who want to learn practical skills quickly and start working. Students should think about what they like to do before they choose.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: How long does university education usually last?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'University education usually lasts for three to four years.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: Which of these jobs is mentioned as a university path?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'This path is perfect for people who want to become doctors, teachers, or managers.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What do vocational students use to practice their skills?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 states: 'Instead, they practice their skills in workshops with real tools.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: Why is it easy for vocational graduates to find a job?

options.C: Many companies need workers with practical skills.

questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 states: 'Today, many companies need workers with good practical skills, so it is easy for vocational graduates to find a job.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: University education is usually cheaper than vocational training.

options.TRUE: If the statement agrees with the information
options.FALSE: If the statement contradicts the information
options.NOT GIVEN: If there is no information on this

questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 states: 'First, it [vocational training] is usually much cheaper than university.' This means university is more expensive, so the statement is False.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Vocational courses usually take only one or two years.

options.TRUE: If the statement agrees with the information
options.FALSE: If the statement contradicts the information
options.NOT GIVEN: If there is no information on this

questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 states: 'These courses are much shorter, usually taking only one or two years.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Tom went to university to study computer science.

options.TRUE: If the statement agrees with the information
options.FALSE: If the statement contradicts the information
options.NOT GIVEN: If there is no information on this

questions.explanation: Paragraph 5 states: 'Tom went to university to study computer science.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Anna wants to open her own restaurant in the future.

options.TRUE: If the statement agrees with the information
options.FALSE: If the statement contradicts the information
options.NOT GIVEN: If there is no information on this

questions.explanation: Paragraph 5 mentions that Anna works as a chef in a restaurant, but there is no information about her wanting to open her own restaurant in the future.

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: University students spend a lot of time reading books and writing __________.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'They spend a lot of time reading books, writing essays, and listening to lectures...'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 states: 'These schools focus on practical skills.'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Anna studied cooking and now works as a __________ in a restaurant.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 5 states: 'Anna went to a vocational school to study cooking... and now works as a chef in a restaurant.'

vocabulary.meaning: education at a college or university level
training that teaches you the skills you need to do a particular job
a short piece of writing on a particular subject
relating to experience, real situations, or hands-on actions rather than ideas
a room or building in which things are made or repaired using tools
a person who has finished their school, college, or university studies

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài cho trình độ A1-A2

1. Dạng bài Multiple Choice (Trắc nghiệm): Các câu hỏi ở trình độ này thường lấy thông tin trực tiếp từ bài đọc. Hãy tìm các từ khóa quan trọng trong câu hỏi (ví dụ: 'how long', 'which jobs') rồi định vị đoạn văn có chứa từ khóa đó để chọn đáp án chính xác nhất.

2. Dạng bài True/False/Not Given: Hãy chú ý rằng các câu hỏi sử dụng từ ngữ gần như giống hệt trong bài khóa. Nếu thông tin trong câu hỏi trái ngược hoàn toàn với bài đọc, đáp án là FALSE. Nếu thông tin hoàn toàn không được nhắc tới (như dự định tương lai của Anna), đáp án chắc chắn là NOT GIVEN.

3. Dạng bài Sentence Completion (Điền từ vào chỗ trống): Đọc kỹ giới hạn từ cho phép (NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS - không quá hai từ). Tìm câu chứa thông tin tương ứng trong bài đọc và sao chép chính xác từ cần điền mà không thay đổi dạng từ.
